175→ """Test first-time worktree creation gets only datetime suffix.""" 176→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 177→ 178→ repo_dir = tmp_path / "erks" 179→ repo_dir.mkdir() 180→ 181→ git_ops = RealGit() 182→ result = ensure_unique_worktree_name("my-feature", repo_dir, git_ops) 183→ 184→ # Should have datetime suffix in format -YY-MM-DD-HHMM 185→ date_suffix = _get_current_date_suffix() 186→ assert result == f"my-feature-{date_suffix}" 187→ assert not (repo_dir / result).exists() 188→ 189→ 190→def test_ensure_unique_worktree_name_duplicate_same_minute(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 191→ """Test duplicate worktree in same minute adds -2 after datetime suffix.""" 192→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 193→ 194→ repo_dir = tmp_path / "erks" 195→ repo_dir.mkdir() 196→ 197→ date_suffix = _get_current_date_suffix() 198→ existing_name = f"my-feature-{date_suffix}" 199→ (repo_dir / existing_name).mkdir() 200→ 201→ git_ops = RealGit() 202→ result = ensure_unique_worktree_name("my-feature", repo_dir, git_ops) 203→ 204→ assert result == f"my-feature-{date_suffix}-2"
... [55 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
These tests in `test_naming.py` call `ensure_unique_worktree_name` directly (not via CLI) and don't pass `now=`, so it falls back to `datetime.now()`. The tests also use `_get_current_date_suffix()` which uses `datetime.now()`. Since both use the same source (`datetime.now()`), these tests are still internally consistent — both sides call `datetime.now()` at roughly the same time.
However, these tests are still flaky at minute boundaries. Since they call `ensure_unique_worktree_name` without passing `now`, the function falls back to `datetime.now()`. I should fix these tests too to pass an explicit `now` parameter, making them fully deterministic:

tool_use.name: Read
tool_use.param: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/core/utils/test_naming.py

session.tool_result:
170→def test_strip_plan_from_filename(value: str, expected: str) -> None: 171→ assert strip_plan_from_filename(value) == expected 172→ 173→ 174→def test_ensure_unique_worktree_name_first_time(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 175→ """Test first-time worktree creation gets only datetime suffix.""" 176→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 177→ 178→ repo_dir = tmp_path / "erks" 179→ repo_dir.mkdir() 180→ 181→ git_ops = RealGit() 182→ result = ensure_unique_worktree_name("my-feature", repo_dir, git_ops) 183→ 184→ # Should have datetime suffix in format -YY-MM-DD-HHMM 185→ date_suffix = _get_current_date_suffix() 186→ assert result == f"my-feature-{date_suffix}" 187→ assert not (repo_dir / result).exists() 188→ 189→ 190→def test_ensure_unique_worktree_name_duplicate_same_minute(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 191→ """Test duplicate worktree in same minute adds -2 after datetime suffix.""" 192→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 193→ 194→ repo_dir = tmp_path / "erks" 195→ repo_dir.mkdir() 196→ 197→ date_suffix = _get_current_date_suffix() 198→ existing_name = f"my-feature-{date_suffix}" 199→ (repo_dir / existing_name).mkdir()
... [55 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: These unit tests directly call `ensure_unique_worktree_name` and also use `datetime.now()` for the expected value. They're also flaky at minute boundaries (the same root cause). Let me fix them by passing an explicit `now` parameter:
